title: JIT recompiles gradient body per fresh closure identity — memoize JIT on closure code/AST not runtime identity
goal: Noesis nested-AD report Issue 3. `(gradient (lambda (x) ...) p)` where the lambda is CREATED INSIDE A LOOP is O(steps * compile): each fresh closure runtime-identity triggers a fresh JIT compile of the gradient body. Hoisting the closure outside the loop removed a >120s hang. Fix: memoize JIT compilation keyed on the closure's CODE/AST (structural identity), not its runtime allocation identity, so repeatedly differentiating structurally-identical closures compiles once. Relates to ESH-0044 (AD throughput).
next_actions: Identify where gradient triggers JIT compilation of the differentiated body and what key is used; switch the cache key to the lambda's AST/code pointer (or a structural hash) rather than the runtime closure value.
Verify a loop that creates a fresh lambda each iteration and differentiates it compiles once, not per-iteration.
acceptance: loop creating fresh structurally-identical gradient lambda compiles once (no per-step recompile), large step counts no longer hang
no correctness regression in AD results
verification: timing test: 100-step loop differentiating an in-loop lambda finishes in O(1) compiles
campaign_payoff: Makes iterative meta-learning loops practical (perf), complements ESH-0070 (correctness) and ESH-0044 (throughput).
